name: Chicken Florentine
instructions: 1. Stir together the all-purpose flour and the kosher salt in a medium bowl. Add the chicken breast cutlets, one at a time, and toss to coat evenly with the flour. Shake off the excess flour and set aside on a plate. 2. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the unsalted butter and the olive oil; cook, swirling occasionally, until the butter is frothy, about 30 seconds. Add the chicken in an even layer; cook, turning once halfway through, until browned and a thermometer inserted into the thickest portion of the chicken breast registers 165 degrees Fahrenheit, about 8 minutes. Transfer the chicken to a wire rack. 3. Add the white wine to the skillet; cook over high heat, stirring occasionally and scraping the bottom to release brown bits, until reduced by half, about 1 minute. Stir in the chicken broth, the heavy cream, the black pepper, the garlic powder, the dried oregano, the dried thyme, and the remaining kosher salt. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat; cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened, about 4 minutes. 4. Stir in the baby spinach; cook, stirring often, until the spinach is wilted, about 1 minute. 5. Return the chicken to the skillet. Cook over medium-high heat, turning the chicken once, until the chicken is heated through, about 1 minute. Remove from heat; add the fresh basil and the Parmesan, swirling to combine.
simpleInstructions: 1. Coat the chicken in the flour and the salt. Sear in the butter and the oil. 2. Reduce the wine in the skillet. Add the broth, the cream, and the spices. Thicken the sauce. 3. Wilt the spinach in the sauce, return the chicken, and finish with the basil and the Parmesan.
